title: Peppery Creamy Argentinian Figs Stew
description: A fragrant, peppery stew of fresh figs in a creamy yogurt sauce, cooked in a clay comal for a brunch‑style Argentinian treat.
instructions: Heat the comal over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the olive oil and swirl to coat the surface.
Sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Toss in the smoked paprika, cumin, cayenne, and a pinch of salt, cooking for another minute to bloom the spices.
Add the sliced figs and a splash of water, covering them lightly and letting them simmer for about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Reduce heat to low and stir in the Greek yogurt, lemon juice, and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Cook gently for an additional 5 minutes, allowing the yogurt to thicken and coat the figs.
Transfer the stew to a serving dish, garnishing with fresh herbs if desired.
Serve hot with crusty bread or warm tortillas for a brunch‑style meal.
